formulas.name: Heckscher-Ohlin Theorem
formulas.formula: Countries will specialize in the production of goods in which they have a comparative advantage.
formulas.notes: An important principle in international trade, stating that countries will export goods in which they have a comparative advantage.

formulas.name: Prebisch-Singer Hypothesis
formulas.formula: Prices of primary commodities fall over time relative to manufactured goods.
formulas.notes: An hypothesis stating that the prices of primary commodities tend to fall over time relative to manufactured goods.

formulas.name: Law of Comparative Advantage
formulas.formula: A country should specialize in producing goods in which it has a comparative advantage.
formulas.notes: A fundamental principle in international trade, stating that countries should specialize in producing goods in which they have a comparative advantage.

formulas.name: Gains from Trade
formulas.formula: Countries can achieve higher output and consumption levels through trade.
formulas.notes: A key concept in international trade, stating that countries can achieve higher output and consumption levels through trade.

formulas.name: Trade Multiplier
formulas.formula: The total increase in GDP resulting from an increase in exports.
formulas.notes: A measure of the impact of trade on a country's GDP.

formulas.name: Terms of Trade
formulas.formula: The ratio of the price of exports to the price of imports.
formulas.notes: A measure of the price competitiveness of a country's exports relative to its imports.
